title: Best AppSumo Deals in 2026: Lifetime Software Actually Worth Buying
desc: The best AppSumo lifetime deals in 2026, re-verified live in late June: top picks for agencies and lead gen, a checklist to avoid duds, and when to buy for an extra 10% off.
keywords: best appsumo deals, best appsumo deals 2026, appsumo lifetime deals, are appsumo lifetime deals worth it, best appsumo deals for agencies, appsumo plus worth it
cta: Want a lead-gen stack that actually converts?
body:
Quick answer: AppSumo lifetime deals are legit, and in 2026 they are still the cheapest way to build a software stack for an agency or small business. You pay once, usually $29 to $99, instead of a monthly subscription. The platform has run for 16 years, serves over 2 million customers, and backs every purchase with a 60-day money-back guarantee. The catch is product survival: independent deal trackers estimate that a large share of lifetime-deal products, sometimes cited around 40 percent, fade or shut down within a few years. So the game is simple. Buy proven tools with strong ratings and a real track record, test them hard inside the 60-day refund window, and never buy on roadmap promises. Below are the picks that pass that filter, re-verified live on AppSumo in late June 2026, plus the exact checklist we use before spending a dollar.

What AppSumo is and why the deals are so cheap

AppSumo is the largest marketplace for software lifetime deals. Early-stage SaaS companies list their product at a steep one-time price, often 80 to 95 percent below the equivalent yearly subscription, in exchange for a burst of users, cash flow, and feedback. You get lifetime access to the plan you bought. The vendor gets distribution. AppSumo takes a cut.

That trade explains both the upside and the risk. The discount is real because the vendor is buying growth, not running a charity. But some of those vendors will pivot, get acquired, or shut down, and a lifetime deal only lasts as long as the product does. AppSumo reduces the risk in three ways: a 60-day money-back guarantee on practically everything, an AppSumo Select badge for products the team vets and partners with directly, and public review scores from verified buyers, rated out of five tacos.

How to pick a lifetime deal that lasts

We apply five filters before buying any deal. They will not catch every dud, but they remove most of them.

  • Rating of 4.5 or higher with at least 50 reviews. A 4.9 with 12 reviews is noise. A 4.7 with 300 reviews is signal.
  • The product has been on the market 12 months or more. Most lifetime-deal failures happen early. A tool that has shipped updates for a year or two is far more likely to be around in year five.
  • An active changelog or public roadmap. If the last update was eight months ago, assume the team has moved on.
  • Buy for what the tool does today. Roadmap promises are marketing. If the current feature set does not justify the price, pass.
  • Stress-test inside the 60-day window. Import real data, run a real campaign, hit the limits of your tier. Refund without guilt if it disappoints.

One more rule that saves money: check whether the deal is tiered. Most AppSumo listings sell License Tier 1, 2, and 3. Buy the lowest tier that covers your usage, because you can usually upgrade later, and tier limits are where cheap deals quietly become expensive.

Browse the current AppSumo deals here

The best live AppSumo deals for agencies and lead gen in 2026

We re-verified every pick below directly on AppSumo on June 26, 2026. Prices are the standing one-time prices and availability is current as of that date; ratings are recent at the time of writing. Deals rotate fast, so if one is gone when you look, the checklist above still applies to whatever replaced it.

  • LeadRocks, $79 one-time (about 95 percent off). Rated 4.5 from over 360 reviews, and AppSumo Select. A B2B contact database with 100M+ records, plus emails, phone numbers, and LinkedIn profiles, a Chrome extension, CSV and CRM export, and built-in email verification. For an agency or a sales team that wants a cheap, owned prospecting list, this is the standout lead-gen deal live right now. The honest catch: like every low-cost database, data accuracy varies, so treat the output as a starting list and verify contacts before you send.
  • FlexiFunnels, $49 one-time (regularly $199). AI-built landing pages, funnels, course pages, and a built-in LMS from a prompt. Pair it with a cold email engine and you have a complete top-of-funnel for under $100.
  • Heffl, $59 one-time (regularly $960). An all-in-one CRM that covers deals, projects, quotes, and invoicing, a genuine option for a small agency that is not ready for a $297 per month platform. Note: it is now in AppSumo's ending-soon list, so it may not be around much longer.
  • Pretty Prompt, $34 one-time (regularly $180). Rated 4.86 with over 5,200 purchases, one of the highest-rated tools on the platform. One-click prompt refinement that makes ChatGPT and Claude outputs noticeably better. At $34 it pays for itself the first time it saves a marketer an hour.
  • Letterly, $89 one-time (regularly $200). Speech-to-structured-text in any app. Founders who think out loud use it to turn voice notes into proposals, follow-up emails, and LinkedIn posts.
  • Snoooz, $39 one-time (regularly $299). An AI email assistant that answers, categorizes, and routes inbound conversations. Useful for shared inboxes where leads currently sit unanswered for hours.
  • Mini Course Generator, $99 one-time (regularly $696). Builds interactive mini-courses with AI. A clean lead-magnet machine for coaches, consultants, and agencies selling expertise.

Proof that deals rotate: FuseBase, a 4.7-rated client-portal tool that was one of the strongest picks on this list earlier in June, has since sold out and now shows only a notify-me button. That is the lifetime-deal market in one example: strong deals disappear, so when one clears the five-filter checklist above, buy it and use the 60-day refund window rather than waiting. New AI tools with launch buzz but only a couple dozen reviews can be great early buys, but they fail our 50-review filter, so watch them and let the review count build.

The always-available picks: AppSumo's own tools

AppSumo also builds and owns a small family of products, including TidyCal for scheduling, SendFox for email newsletters, and BreezeDoc for e-signatures. These are permanent listings rather than rotating deals, and they carry the lowest survival risk on the platform, because AppSumo is not going to shut down its own products. TidyCal in particular has become the default Calendly replacement for lifetime-deal buyers. If you want zero-drama utility software for the boring parts of your stack, start there.

When to buy: Sumo Day, Black Friday, and AppSumo Plus

Timing matters more than most buyers realize. AppSumo drops new deals every week, but a few times a year it runs sitewide sales where everything gets cheaper. Sumo Day, its biggest annual event, ran in early June 2026 and has now wrapped, so the next sitewide moments to watch are AppSumo's mid-year promotions and Black Friday in November, which is historically the deepest discount of the year. You rarely need to wait, though: new deals land weekly and the 60-day refund means buying today carries little risk. If a deal you want happens to be live during a sitewide sale, that is the moment to stack the savings.

The other lever is AppSumo Plus at $99 per year. Members get 10 percent off every purchase, extended access to expiring deals, and members-only offers. The math is simple: if you expect to spend more than $990 a year on deals, Plus pays for itself. A founder buying two or three tools a year should skip it. An agency building a full stack in one quarter should join for that quarter. Plus members also get a stronger safety net, including full credit back if a Select tool shuts down within its first year.

See what is live on AppSumo right now

The honest downsides nobody puts in the headline

We earn a commission if you buy through our links, so take this section as the proof that we want you buying well, not buying often.

  • Lifetime means the product's lifetime, not yours. If the vendor shuts down, your deal dies with it. This is the single biggest risk, and it is why the checklist above exists.
  • Tier limits bite later. The $49 tier that covers you today may not cover you at twice the volume, and upgrade pricing is not always generous.
  • Support is rarely subscription-grade. You paid once, and some vendors quietly prioritize monthly subscribers in the support queue.
  • Data and feature gaps versus category leaders are real. A $79 contact database is not ZoomInfo, a $59 CRM is not HubSpot, and a $49 funnel builder is not ClickFunnels. For core revenue infrastructure, sometimes the subscription is the right call.
  • Sold out means sold out. Deals are limited runs, as FuseBase just showed. Hesitating on a strong deal usually costs more than a refunded experiment.

Building a real stack: where lifetime deals fit

The smartest way to use AppSumo is to cover the supporting layer of your business for a few hundred dollars, one time, and keep your spend for the systems that directly produce revenue. Scheduling, client portals, documents, internal email, and content tooling are perfect lifetime-deal territory. The pipeline itself, meaning the system that finds prospects, reaches them, and books calls, is usually worth doing properly with dedicated infrastructure.

That is the part we build for clients every day. If you want the pipeline handled end to end, our team runs done-for-you B2B lead generation and cold email campaigns, wired into the AI automation that keeps them running, and they plug straight into whatever stack you assemble, lifetime deals included. Buy the $79 contact database on AppSumo, and spend what you saved on the system that turns it into booked calls.

Bottom line: AppSumo in 2026 rewards disciplined buyers. Use the five-filter checklist, favor proven tools with real review volume, time big purchases to Sumo Day or Black Friday, and treat the 60-day guarantee as a free trial. Do that, and a few hundred dollars buys you a stack that would otherwise cost thousands a year.

faqs: Are AppSumo lifetime deals legit in 2026?

Yes. AppSumo has operated for 16 years, serves over 2 million customers, and backs nearly every purchase with a 60-day money-back guarantee. The deals are real lifetime licenses sold by software vendors who want fast distribution. The genuine risk is not fraud but product survival: some vendors shut down or pivot, and a lifetime deal only lasts as long as the product does, so buy proven tools with strong review volume.
What does lifetime actually mean on AppSumo?

Lifetime means the lifetime of the product, not your lifetime. You get access to the plan you bought for as long as the vendor operates that product. If the company shuts down or sunsets the tool, the deal ends with it. That is why experienced buyers favor products that are at least a year old, hold a 4.5 plus rating with 50 or more reviews, and show an active changelog.
What are the best AppSumo deals right now in 2026?

Re-verified live on June 26, 2026, the strongest picks for agencies and small businesses are LeadRocks at $79 for a 100M+ B2B contact database, FlexiFunnels at $49 for AI-built funnels, Heffl at $59 as an all-in-one CRM (now ending soon), Pretty Prompt at $34 for better AI outputs, Letterly at $89 for voice-to-text, Snoooz at $39 for AI inbound email, and Mini Course Generator at $99 for lead-magnet courses. FuseBase, a popular pick earlier in June, has sold out, which shows how fast deals rotate, so check current listings before buying.
How do I avoid buying an AppSumo deal that shuts down?

Apply five filters: a rating of 4.5 or higher with at least 50 reviews, a product that has been live for 12 months or more, an active changelog or roadmap, a price justified by current features rather than promises, and a hard test of the tool inside the 60-day refund window with real data. AppSumo Select badged products are also vetted more closely by AppSumo itself.
Is AppSumo Plus worth $99 a year?

AppSumo Plus gives you 10 percent off every purchase, extended time on expiring deals, and members-only offers. It pays for itself once you spend about $990 a year on deals. If you are building out a full software stack in one go, join for that period and stack the discount with sitewide sales like Sumo Day or Black Friday. If you only buy one or two small deals a year, skip it.
Can I get a refund on AppSumo?

Yes. AppSumo offers a 60-day money-back guarantee on almost all lifetime deals, and refunds are processed from your account dashboard without needing to justify the request. The smart approach is to treat those 60 days as a free trial: import real data, run a real project, and push the limits of your license tier. If the tool falls short, refund it well before the window closes.
